q.en: What animal family do giant pandas belong to?
q.zh: 大熊猫属于哪个动物家族？

explain.en: The book says, "Members of the bear family, giant pandas are easily recognizable because of their thick black-and-white fur."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“作为熊类家族的成员，大熊猫因其厚厚的黑白相间的皮毛而极易辨认。”

q.en: Where do wild giant pandas live today?
q.zh: 如今野生大熊猫生活在哪里？

options.en: The forest-covered mountains of western China
options.zh: 中国西部森林覆盖的山区

explain.en: The book says, "Wild giant pandas remain in only one place on Earth—the forest-covered mountains of western China."
explain.zh: 书中说：“野生大熊猫只存在于地球上的一个地方——中国西部森林覆盖的山区。”

q.en: What does the name 'panda' mean, and what makes up almost all of a panda's diet?
q.zh: “panda”这个名字是什么意思？大熊猫的食物几乎全部由什么构成？

explain.en: The book says, "Panda means 'bamboo eater,' and bamboo makes up almost all of a panda's diet."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“panda意为‘吃竹子者’，竹子几乎构成大熊猫饮食的全部。”

q.en: How much bamboo does an adult panda eat each day?
q.zh: 一只成年大熊猫每天要吃多少竹子？

options.en: As many as 16 hours a day, up to 18 kg (40 lbs)
options.zh: 每天多达16小时，高达18公斤（40磅）

options.en: About 1 hour a day, up to 2 kg
Two meals a day, 50 kg total

explain.en: The book says, "An adult panda spends as many as sixteen hours a day eating up to 18 kilograms (40 lbs) of bamboo."
explain.zh: 书中说：“成年大熊猫每天花多达16小时进食，吃掉高达18公斤（40磅）的竹子。”

q.en: Do giant pandas hibernate like other bears do?
q.zh: 大熊猫会像其他熊那样冬眠吗？

options.zh: 会，它们整个冬天都在睡

explain.en: The book says, "Pandas do not hibernate like other bears do. They must eat year-round to get enough nutrients."
explain.zh: 书中说：“大熊猫不像其他熊那样冬眠。它们必须全年进食以获取足够营养。”

q.zh: 它们为什么被称为“大熊猫”？

options.en: To distinguish them from red pandas
Because they are the largest bears
Because they eat the most food
Because they live in giant trees

explain.en: The book says, "They are called 'giant pandas' to distinguish them from red pandas, which also live in bamboo forests in China."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“它们被称为‘大熊猫’，是为了和同样生活在中国竹林里的小熊猫区分开来。”

q.en: Is the red panda a close relative of the giant panda?
q.zh: 小熊猫是大熊猫的近亲吗？

options.en: No, red pandas are relatives of raccoons
options.zh: 不是，小熊猫是浣熊的亲戚

options.en: Yes, they are the same animal
options.zh: 是的，它们是同一种动物

options.en: No, red pandas are relatives of cats
options.zh: 不是，小熊猫是猫的亲戚

explain.en: The book says, "The red panda is not a close relative of the giant panda... Red pandas are relatives of raccoons."
explain.zh: 书中说：“小熊猫并不是大熊猫的近亲……小熊猫是浣熊的亲戚。”

q.en: Why have giant pandas become a symbol mentioned in the book?
q.zh: 书中说大熊猫为何成了某种象征？

options.en: Because they are rare and endangered, symbolizing the effort to protect Earth's animals
options.zh: 因为它们稀有且濒危，象征着保护地球动物的努力

options.en: Because they are the strongest animal
options.zh: 因为它们是最强壮的动物

explain.en: The book says, "They are so rare and endangered that they have become a symbol of the effort to protect Earth's animals."
explain.zh: 书中说：“它们如此稀有且濒危，已成为保护地球动物这一努力的象征。”

q.en: About how many giant pandas are left in the wild, and how many preserves has China created?
q.zh: 野生大熊猫大约还剩多少只？中国建立了多少个保护区？

options.en: About 1,000 in the wild, with thirty-four preserves
options.zh: 野外约1000只，建立了34个保护区

explain.en: The book says scientists believe "only about 1,000 giant pandas are still alive in the wild," and China "has created thirty-four preserves to protect their habitat."
explain.zh: 书中说科学家认为“野外仅存约1000只大熊猫”，而中国“已建立34个保护区来保护它们的栖息地”。

q.en: How does a panda mark the edges of its home range?
q.zh: 大熊猫如何标记自己活动范围的边界？

options.en: It rubs a smelly liquid on tree trunks and scratches the bark
options.zh: 它在树干上蹭有气味的液体并抓挠树皮

options.en: It builds a wall of bamboo

explain.en: The book says, "One sign is a smelly liquid that the panda releases from its body and rubs on tree trunks. A panda also marks the edges of its home range by scratching the bark of tree trunks."
explain.zh: 书中说：“一个标记是熊猫从体内排出的有气味液体，它把液体蹭在树干上。熊猫还通过抓挠树干来标记活动范围的边界。”

prompt.en: The book says giant pandas are rare and endangered, but people around the world are working hard to keep them alive—through preserves, breeding programs, and laws. Why do you think people care so much about saving one animal? What could you do to help protect an endangered animal?
prompt.zh: 书中说大熊猫稀有且濒危，但世界各地的人们通过保护区、繁育计划和立法努力保护它们。你认为人们为何如此在意拯救一个物种？你能为保护濒危动物做些什么？

en: Giant pandas are a symbol of protecting Earth's animals.
Only about 1,000 remain in the wild.
China created 34 preserves and has strict laws.
Think about what you value and small actions you could take.
zh: 大熊猫是保护地球动物的象征。
中国建立了34个保护区并制定严格法律。
想想你珍视什么，以及你能采取的小行动。
